## Development and Maturation


1. Development means the **growth** people experience throughout their lives.
2. It encapsulates social, cognitive, emotional, and physical growth. 
3. It is defined by **milestones**.

****

<callout type="example">* In infancy, crawling, talking, and walking are deemed as milestones.
* Puberty may be considered a developmental milestone for adolescents.</callout>



<callout type="warning">* Development does not stop once someone is an adult, it actually continues until death.
* Our bodies are constantly changing, even though we have become adults.</callout>

![An image displaying the stages of growth and development.](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/ca189c821b2039a0f43670437ad7ed5a2d314ecc-1920x1080.png)

## Determinism


1. **Determinism** is the belief that all behaviour is due to **underlying factors**.
2. These factors can be **biological** or **environmental**.
3. It is important to understand the influence of both biology and environment in human behaviour.

<callout type="example">* Someone who believes in biological determinism may attribute a diagnosis of depression to genetics or neurotransmitters.
* Someone else who believes in environmental determinism may attribute this diagnosis to their environment (upbringing, socioeconomic status, etc.)</callout>

****

## Reflection


<callout type="self_review">1. When does development stop?
2. Define determinism in the context of psychology.</callout>
